title: Swedish Market Research: Employee Benefit Platform
subtitle: Exploring market appetite and organizational benefit needs across Swedish employer segments
challenge: A leading European provider of employee benefits was navigating a period of intense internal transformation — significant global changes within the organization were creating pressure to accelerate market expansion. The Swedish market represented a strategic growth opportunity, but the company lacked validated insight into whether its multi-benefit platform — offering lunch, sports, wellness, commuting, and culture benefits through a single employer app — would genuinely resonate with Swedish employers. The research challenge was twofold: assess market appetite for a comprehensive, multi-benefit one-stop-shop model, and surface deep insight into how Swedish organizations would manage and prioritize benefits as part of their long-term strategies.

designProcess.intro: I owned this study from start to finish — scoping the research agenda, building and leading a research team, coaching colleagues on interview methodology, and maintaining continuous communication with the stakeholder group throughout. Working through a period of significant organizational turbulence, I structured the engagement to deliver both strategic clarity and actionable GTM direction, grounding every recommendation in evidence from Swedish employers rather than internal assumptions.

steps.title: Stakeholder Alignment & Research Scoping
steps.description: Collaborated with internal stakeholders to surface existing assumptions about the Swedish market and benefit procurement landscape. Mapped organizational hypotheses against known unknowns, then translated these into a structured research agenda designed to address both immediate GTM questions and longer-term strategic priorities around how employers will manage and value benefits.

steps.title: User Interviews — HR Managers & Employer Decision-Makers
steps.description: Planned, recruited for, and conducted in-depth interviews with HR managers and employer decision-makers across Swedish companies of varying size and industry. Sessions explored current benefit management practices, willingness to consolidate under a single platform, and the role of employee benefits in retention and engagement strategies. I led recruitment to ensure sample quality and representativeness across segments.

steps.title: Analysis, Synthesis & Insights Delivery
steps.description: Ran comprehensive analysis of interview findings, identifying cross-segment patterns and surfacing tensions between organizational aspirations and current benefit management maturity. Synthesized findings into an actionable insights report, presented directly to the stakeholder team, with strategic recommendations designed to guide go-to-market positioning and market entry priorities for Sweden.

insights: Swedish employers showed strong appetite for a consolidated benefit platform, but purchasing intent was closely tied to administrative simplicity — complexity in setup or management was a recurring blocker
Employee wellbeing was increasingly framed as a strategic retention lever, particularly in competitive talent markets — benefit comprehensiveness was beginning to function as a hiring differentiator
Awareness of available benefits among employees was systematically low across segments, pointing to an adoption loop problem that platform design alone would not solve
Mid-size employers (approx. 100–500 employees) showed the clearest pain around benefit administration overhead, making them the most immediately addressable segment for initial GTM focus

images.alt: Research synthesis workshop with affinity mapping

deliverables.intro: The engagement produced a comprehensive research report capturing market appetite signals, segment-level findings, and organizational need patterns across the Swedish employer landscape. The final deliverable was an actionable insights report presented to the stakeholder team, structured to directly inform go-to-market strategy, segment prioritization, and positioning decisions for the Swedish market entry.

images.alt: Segment opportunity matrix showing PMF strength across employer verticals
Positioning recommendation framework derived from research synthesis

results.intro: The study gave the company a validated evidence base for its Swedish market strategy — replacing internal assumptions with structured segment insight and directional GTM recommendations grounded in employer reality.
